license_status.label: Active
license_status.message: Approved for oriental.reputifly.cloud with license key RSPD-C5EE-****-1BE8.

authentication.note: Generate or regenerate the primary key from the Agentic AI screen before handing this profile to an agent.

read.cpt_items: /custom-post-types/{slug}/items (query: page, per_page, status, search, post_type auto-forced; for Tier 2 filters see filters[...] below)
read.template_slot_verify: /templates/{slot}/verify (v1.59.0 — server-side live verification: fetches a matching URL, checks fingerprint, detects theme wrappers, reports Cloudflare/host cache status, returns probable_causes + next_steps on failure)
read.template_slot_info: Slots: single-product, archive-product, single-post, archive-post, front-page, page, cart, checkout, 0, search. v1.59.0 introduces PIXEL-PERFECT isolation for every template slot. Three render modes on POST /templates/{slot}: (1) default / compat — theme CSS loads, wp_head fires (legacy behavior, unchanged). (2) "isolated": true — PURE isolation: zero theme, zero wp_head, zero CSS bleed. Your template is the whole HTML response (same guarantee as deploy_raw_page). Best for single-{cpt} / archive-{cpt} / single-post / archive-post / blog / portfolio / property / doctor directories where you control every pixel. (3) "isolated": true + "enable_wp_head": true — isolated PHP + normal wp_head()/wp_footer() firing. Use for single-product when add-to-cart AJAX, cart fragments, SEO plugins (Yoast/Rank Math) need to emit. Theme files still skipped; only action hooks fire. (4) "isolated": true + "enqueue_wc_frontend": true — lean WooCommerce mode: enqueues only wc-add-to-cart, wc-cart-fragments, wc-single-product, jquery. Add-to-cart works without full wp_head. ALSO: "dequeue_theme_styles": true (default when isolated) strips theme CSS handles. Add "strict": false (unused here). After every POST, call GET /templates/{slot}/verify to confirm the render is live and not being served stale by OPcache / a page cache / Cloudflare edge. If verify reports fingerprint_hit=false, call POST /templates/{slot}/force-refresh to beat OPcache + POST /cache/flush to beat page/edge caches. DYNAMIC DATA: inside the template PHP, $wp_query is already populated. Use the_title(), the_content(), get_the_ID(), get_post_meta(get_the_ID(), '{field_name}', true), the_post() / have_posts() loop, woocommerce_template_single_price() etc. cart and checkout slots intentionally DO NOT support isolated mode — they depend on WC sessions, nonces, and payment gateways that require the full WC template stack. FLAG PERSISTENCE (v1.59.1): flags (isolated, enable_wp_head, enqueue_wc_frontend, dequeue_theme_styles) inherit from the prior saved state when omitted from a POST — so small incremental updates keep your config. If you want to reset them all to default, pass "reset_flags": true in the payload. The POST response includes a "flag_sources" map showing exactly where each flag's current value came from ("payload", "inherited", or "default") — check that if you're unsure why a flag is set. LAYOUT INJECTIONS (v1.59.2): stored via POST /content/layout-injections with template_slots:[this_slot] are now automatically applied to the rendered output for BOTH isolated renders (buffer-spliced — header_html after <body>, body_html + footer_html before </body>) and regular theme renders (wp_head + wp_body_open + wp_footer hooks). No action needed in your template code — just POST your chrome once via layout-injections and it appears on every targeted slot. THEME LEAK PROTECTION (v1.59.2): in any isolated mode (pure / with_wp_head / with_wc_scripts), theme-sourced body classes (wp-theme-*, hello-elementor, elementor-default, elementor-page-*) are auto-stripped from body_class() / get_body_class() output, and theme-origin <link>/<script>/<style> tags (everything under /wp-content/themes/) are regex-stripped from wp_head()/wp_footer() output. Pixel-perfect guarantee survives theme swaps forever. COMPAT-MODE WARNING (v1.59.2): if you POST a bare compat template (isolated:false + no get_header/wp_head/<html>/header_html), the response includes a warnings[] entry with fix_options. WP will include() the file as-is and render a naked fragment — probably not what you want.
read.public_wordpress_cpt_rest: /wp-json/wp/v2/{post_type} (RSPD custom post types include rspd_fields for structured custom media and scalar fields)
read.debug_errors: /debug/errors?lines=100&search=... (PHP debug log — requires WP_DEBUG_LOG=true in wp-config; usually off in production)
read.debug_rspd_log: /debug/rspd-log?level=error|warning|info&context=&since=&limit=100 (v1.60.2 — RSPD plugin event ring buffer, persists across requests, ALWAYS works regardless of WP_DEBUG_LOG. Captures every defensive try/catch, attribute save warning, dropdown repair fallback, cache flush failure, isolated-render exception. NOTE: only logs when an exception is THROWN — normal WP_Error validation returns are NOT captured. Response keys: total, capacity, in_buffer_total, counts, entries[], note. DELETE to clear. v1.60.3: POST /debug/rspd-log/test to manually ping the buffer round-trip.)
read.ecom_payment_config: /ecommerce/payment-config (METADATA ONLY — gateway publishable keys + SDK URLs for diagnostics. NOT a universal headless checkout engine. Use /ecommerce/checkout-config for production cart/checkout customization.)
read.ecom_checkout_config: /ecommerce/checkout-config (v1.62.6 — FRAGILE BETA, STILL BEING BUILT. Recommended baseline theme: Hello Elementor (best support, fewest layout edge cases). Two modes: (1) "classic_native" (default) — theme renders chrome, plugin styles WC fragments. Best on classic themes. (2) "plugin_shell" — plugin emits complete HTML document, theme bypassed entirely. Works on FSE block themes too but layout container width may differ slightly. Same customization surface for both: design.tokens, checkout.layout (two_column|single_column|stepped), checkout.sections[], checkout.components{order_notes, ship_different_address, coupon, login, place_order_label, trust_badges[], upsells[]}, cart.layout, cart.upsells[], cart.cross_sells, text.{coupon_message, required_field_template, invalid_payment_method, ...} (30+ keys), chrome.header_html/footer_html. plugin_shell adds: shell.{chrome_strategy, chrome_html_top/bottom, page_title_cart, page_title_checkout, max_width}. KNOWN LIMITATIONS: not yet stress-tested on Astra Pro, Divi, Bricks, Avada, Flatsome, Woodmart. Mobile responsive QA pending. Theme-injected inline styles (Divi/Bricks builders) can beat plugin CSS. If issues arise on a non-Hello-Elementor theme, switch to Hello Elementor first to isolate.)
read.ecom_checkout_diagnostics: /ecommerce/checkout-diagnostics (v1.61.0 — STATIC checks: WC active, cart/checkout pages exist, shortcode vs block, gateways enabled, template engine loaded, config persisted. Returns ok/errors[]/warnings[]/fixes[].)
read.ecom_shell_preflight: /ecommerce/checkout-shell/preflight (v1.62.0 — STATIC checks for plugin_shell mode: chrome resolution simulation, theme assets to dequeue, gateway scripts detected. Cheap, idempotent.)
read.ecom_shell_verify: /ecommerce/checkout-shell/verify (v1.62.0 — DYNAMIC verification: fetches live /cart/ + /checkout/ over HTTP, asserts response markers (X-Rspd-Render-Mode header, data-rspd-shell body attr, shell CSS payload, marker comment, WC functional selectors). Returns PASS/FAIL with per-marker breakdown — IRREFUTABLE proof the shell is rendering.)

mutating.layout_injections: /content/layout-injections (v1.63.0 — accepts header_html/body_html/footer_html/content_html/wc_before_thankyou_html/wc_after_thankyou_html plus targeting via page_ids[] AND/OR template_slots[]. NEW v1.63.0: hide_selectors[] — array of CSS selectors to HIDE on every targeted page via display:none + visibility:hidden + height:0 emitted at wp_head priority MAX. Solves "duplicate chrome" / "triple header" — when the active theme natively renders chrome AND you also inject chrome via header_html, list the theme.s selectors here to suppress them. Safety exclusion list prevents hiding form.checkout, #place_order, #wpadminbar, .rspd-shell, etc.)
mutating.layout_injections_verify_hide: /content/layout-injections/verify-hide?url=https://example.com/ (v1.63.0 — IRREFUTABLE proof the hide_selectors feature is live on a target URL. Fetches via wp_remote_get, parses response for the rspd-hide-selectors style block, reports verdict PASS/PARTIAL/FAIL with per-selector DOM-match data. PASS = block emitted + selectors match config + every selector targets at least one element on the page.)
mutating.health_check: /audit/health-check (GET — scans all pages, products, posts, media for issues)
mutating.set_template_force_refresh: /templates/{slot}/force-refresh (v1.59.0 — aggressive re-materialize: delete+rewrite file, invalidate OPcache, bust template cache version. Use when POST /templates/{slot} appears to succeed but a live GET still shows the old render.)
mutating.test_rspd_log: /debug/rspd-log/test (v1.60.3 — POST {level, context, message} optional; writes one entry into the rspd_event_log ring buffer so agents can verify the buffer is alive without provoking a real exception)
mutating.generate_variations: /products/{product_id}/variations/generate (v1.60.0 — response includes skip_details[] with per-skip {combo, reason, existing_variation_id})
mutating.dedupe_variations: /products/{product_id}/variations/dedupe (v1.60.0 — trash variations with no SKU+no price OR duplicates of an existing combo. Body: {dry_run, delete_empty, delete_dupes})
mutating.ecom_checkout_smoke_test: /ecommerce/checkout-smoke-test (v1.61.0 — POST {place_order?:bool, gateway?:"cod", restore_cart?:bool}. DYNAMIC end-to-end check: populates cart with test product, renders cart+checkout, parses for #order_review/#payment/place_order/checkout-nonce. Returns selectors{} object. Optional COD order placement gated to non-production environments OR rspd_ecom_test_mode=1.)

recommended_workflow: List WordPress pages and Archimedes deployments.
Pull current managed code/context before changing existing deployments.
Verify the result summary and updated routes.

deploy_website_pages._render_mode_note: Accepted render_mode values: "isolated" (default, full theme bypass — use this for raw HTML deploys) or "compatibility" (theme wp_head/wp_footer still fire). Legacy aliases: "display_as_is", "raw", "raw_html" all normalize to "isolated" silently. The /deployments/{id}/manifest endpoint always returns the canonical name.

deploy_raw_page.skip_rewrite_links: optional bool — skip <a href> URL rewriting for trusted HTML with correct absolute URLs
deploy_raw_page.verify_live_render: optional bool — if true, the server hits the live page URL and returns a live_render diagnostic in the response (fingerprint_hit, theme_wrapper_detected, ok). Use this when deploying on new client sites to confirm the theme is not intercepting the render.
deploy_raw_page._response_note: Response includes a "warnings" array with link rewrite issues and render_mode coercion notes. When verify_live_render=true, a "live_render" object also appears with diagnostic data.

create_post._doc: Creates a post or CPT item. Use post_type to target any registered type (including RSPD custom post types). For custom fields, send rspd_fields (canonical) — acf_fields is accepted as a legacy alias. If both are sent, rspd_fields wins.
create_post.title: My First Blog Post
create_post.content: Hello world! This is my first blog post.
create_post.excerpt: A short summary of the post.

taxonomies._doc: Custom taxonomy slugs → arrays of term names or IDs. Names auto-created if missing. Only taxonomies attached to this post_type are applied.

rspd_fields._doc: Structured custom field values for RSPD CPTs. Keys must match fields defined via POST /custom-post-types/{slug}/fields. Unknown keys are reported in warnings.rejected_fields.

create_post.acf_fields: (legacy alias of rspd_fields — omit when using rspd_fields)
create_post._response_note: Response includes accepted_fields and warnings.rejected_fields when unknown custom field keys are received (HTTP 200). Pass strict=true to get a 400 instead.

update_post._doc: Patches an existing post/item by ID. All fields are optional; only provided keys change. Accepts the same rspd_fields / acf_fields / taxonomies / strict rules as create_post.
update_post.title: Updated Blog Post Title

bulk_create_posts._doc: Bulk-create posts or CPT items. post_type applies to every item in the batch. Each item accepts the same shape as create_post (without its own post_type).

create_cpt_item._doc: Creates an item in a specific RSPD custom post type. Targets /custom-post-types/{slug}/items — post_type is taken from the URL slug (don't include it in the body). See per-CPT auto-generated templates like create_portfolio_item or create_property_item elsewhere in this profile for concrete field-level examples.
create_cpt_item.title: Example Item

rspd_fields._doc: See the per-CPT create_{slug}_item template for the actual field names and expected types.

taxonomies._doc: Optional. Shape: { tax_slug: ["Term Name" | term_id] }. Terms are auto-created when missing.

bulk_create_cpt_items._doc: POST /custom-post-types/{slug}/items/bulk — create up to 50 items in one request. post_type forced from URL slug.

update_cpt_item._doc: Patch an item in a specific CPT. Targets /custom-post-types/{slug}/items/{post_id}. Same shape as update_post.
update_cpt_item.title: Updated Item

meta._yoast_wpseo_metadesc: Custom meta description for search engines.

bulk_upload_media._mode_1_zip: Upload a ZIP file as multipart "file" field. All images inside are extracted and added to the media library.
bulk_upload_media.set_featured: true (optional — first image becomes featured image for post_id)
bulk_upload_media.set_as: gallery (optional — save all IDs to this meta key on post_id)
bulk_upload_media.append: true (optional — append to existing gallery meta)

set_template._doc: Deploy a template override for any slot: single-product, archive-product, single-post, archive-post, front-page, page, search, 404, single-{cpt_slug}, archive-{cpt_slug}. v1.59 adds PIXEL-PERFECT isolation so you can bypass the theme entirely. Set isolated: true for zero theme CSS / wp_head bleed — same guarantee as deploy_raw_page but for dynamic CPT/product/archive URLs. After POST, call GET /templates/{slot}/verify to confirm the render is live; call POST /templates/{slot}/force-refresh if a stale OPcache bytecode is suspected.
set_template._layout_injections: v1.59.2: if you've POSTed to /content/layout-injections with template_slots:[this_slot] and header_html/body_html/footer_html, that HTML is automatically spliced into the rendered output (after your code). No action needed in this template. Works for isolated AND theme renders. Applied for the whole site's chrome (header with nav/cart, footer credits, site-wide style overrides).
set_template._flag_persistence: Flags (isolated, enable_wp_head, enqueue_wc_frontend, dequeue_theme_styles) INHERIT from the prior saved state of this slot when OMITTED from the current payload. This means small incremental POSTs like {"code": "..."} keep your existing isolated + WC config without re-specifying it. If you want every omitted flag to reset to its default, pass "reset_flags": true. The POST response includes a "flag_sources" map ({isolated, enable_wp_head, ...} → "payload" | "inherited" | "default") — check it when in doubt about why a flag is set.
set_template._theme_leak_protection: v1.59.2: in any isolated mode, "wp-theme-*" body classes and /wp-content/themes/ URLs in head/footer markup are auto-stripped. You never need to hand-dequeue theme assets.
set_template._compat_mode_warning: v1.59.2: if isolated:false AND your code doesn't call get_header()/wp_head() AND has no <html> root AND no header_html wrapper, the POST response returns warnings[] with fix_options because WP will render a naked fragment.
set_template.code: > <?php echo esc_html( get_the_title() ); ?> — <?php bloginfo( 'name' ); ?>

_render_modes.compat_default: Omit isolated (or isolated:false). Theme CSS loads, theme wrappers may appear. Legacy behavior.
_render_modes.isolated_pure: isolated:true (dequeue_theme_styles defaults true). Zero theme, zero wp_head, zero CSS bleed. Your template IS the full HTML response. Best for CPT singles/archives, portfolios, directories, blog posts, front-page rewrites.
_render_modes.isolated_with_wp_head: isolated:true + enable_wp_head:true. wp_head()/wp_footer() fire so SEO plugins (Yoast/RankMath), analytics, WC JS emit normally. Theme files still skipped. Best for single-product when add-to-cart AJAX + variations need the full WC frontend stack.
_render_modes.isolated_with_wc_scripts: isolated:true + enqueue_wc_frontend:true. Lean WC mode — only wc-add-to-cart, wc-cart-fragments, wc-single-product, jquery enqueued. Add-to-cart works without full wp_head. Faster than enable_wp_head.

set_template._response_note: Response includes opcache_invalidated, preview_urls, and verification.endpoint. After this, immediately call GET /templates/{slot}/verify — it fetches a matching URL, looks for a fingerprint from your code, detects theme wrappers, reports Cloudflare/host cache status. If verify fails, response includes probable_causes and next_steps.

create_product.title: Premium Widget
create_product.short_description: A brief summary shown on product cards.

update_product._doc: Update any product field. v1.59.3 accepts type + attributes + default_attributes so variable products can be fully configured from the agent API without wp-admin. Response.changes reports which domains were touched (post_fields, meta, type, attributes, default_attributes, categories, tags).
update_product.title: Updated Widget Name

_variable_product_example._note: To convert simple -> variable and wire a global attribute in ONE call, send type + attributes together. Then call POST /products/{id}/variations/generate to auto-create the variations.

update_product._attribute_shape: Each attribute object: name (required), slug (optional — pa_* for global taxonomy, auto-created via wc_create_attribute if missing), options (array of term names or labels — auto-created as terms for taxonomy attrs), visible (default true), variation (default false — set true for variation-producing attrs), position (int).

update_menus._note: To CREATE a new menu: provide "name" (required, unique). To UPDATE an existing menu: provide "menu_id" (required). Passing both is also fine — menu_id wins. Without either, the request returns rspd_agent_menu_name_required.
update_menus.name: Main Menu

update_wc_email_template.code: Custom HTML here

bulk_create_variations._doc: v1.60.0 — for taxonomy attributes (pa_*) the agent can pass either the term NAME ("Oxblood") or SLUG ("oxblood"); the plugin auto-resolves to slug at save time so duplicate variations with mixed casing can no longer be created. For local (non-taxonomy) attributes case is preserved as-is.

dedupe_variations._doc: v1.60.0 — Trash empty/duplicate variations on a variable product. Run after a buggy /generate or attribute import created stub variations (no SKU + no price) or duplicates with mixed-casing attribute values. Set dry_run:true to preview without acting.

update_wc_payment_gateway.title: Credit Card (Stripe)
update_wc_payment_gateway.description: Pay with your credit card via Stripe.

chatbot_setup.system_prompt: Products: Product A ($29), Product B ($49). Services: Web Design, SEO. Hours: Mon-Fri 9am-6pm. Location: Singapore. Contact: hello@example.com
chatbot_setup.widget_logo: optional URL — custom logo image for chatbot header and bubble (round, 38x38px). Omit for default chat SVG icon.
chatbot_setup.widget_position: optional "bottom-right" (default) or "bottom-left" — controls bubble and panel position.
chatbot_setup.welcome_message: Hi! How can I help you today?
chatbot_setup.fallback_message: We have reached our chat limit for this month. Please contact us directly.
chatbot_setup.widget_page_ids: optional int[] — show chatbot only on these page IDs. Omit or empty array = all pages.
chatbot_setup.client_email: optional email — client notification email. When provided with booking_enabled, enables agentic booking (AI sends confirmation emails).
chatbot_setup.booking_enabled: optional bool — enable AI booking with email confirmations. Requires client_email.

configure_reputifly_email_for_forms._description: To integrate the Reputifly Email API for form notifications, POST to /settings with the rspd_reputifly_email option. The client_email MUST be a registered account in the Reputifly system. The API uses the standard lead-capture path (_to only, NO _send_to). When a customer submits a form, the plugin calls the Reputifly API server-side — no CORS issues, no domain whitelisting needed.
configure_reputifly_email_for_forms.note: The client_email is the _to parameter sent to the Reputifly API. It must be a registered account. The notification email (who actually receives the booking alert) is set separately in rspd_form_config.notification_email.

set_template_single-product._flag_persistence: Flags inherit from prior saved state when omitted. Pass reset_flags:true to force defaults. Response includes flag_sources map so you can see exactly which value source each flag used.
set_template_single-product._layout_injections: v1.59.2: POST your site-wide chrome (header, nav, cart drawer, footer) ONCE to /content/layout-injections with template_slots:["single-product"] — it will auto-appear on every rendered page for this slot. No need to embed chrome in each template.
set_template_single-product._theme_leak_protection: v1.59.2: wp-theme-* body classes and /wp-content/themes/ URLs are auto-stripped in all isolated modes. Pixel-perfect guaranteed.
set_template_single-product._response_note: After POST, call GET /templates/single-product/verify. On failure the response includes probable_causes and next_steps (force-refresh, /cache/flush, Cloudflare purge).
set_template_single-product._doc: PIXEL-PERFECT single-product template. Isolated + wc scripts enqueued so add-to-cart & variations work. For SEO plugins / analytics, also set enable_wp_head: true (wp_head fires but theme files still skipped).
set_template_single-product.code:
> <?php echo esc_html( get_the_title() ); ?>

get_price_html(); ?>

set_template_archive-product._flag_persistence: Flags inherit from prior saved state when omitted. Pass reset_flags:true to force defaults. Response includes flag_sources map so you can see exactly which value source each flag used.
set_template_archive-product._layout_injections: v1.59.2: POST your site-wide chrome (header, nav, cart drawer, footer) ONCE to /content/layout-injections with template_slots:["archive-product"] — it will auto-appear on every rendered page for this slot. No need to embed chrome in each template.
set_template_archive-product._theme_leak_protection: v1.59.2: wp-theme-* body classes and /wp-content/themes/ URLs are auto-stripped in all isolated modes. Pixel-perfect guaranteed.
set_template_archive-product._response_note: After POST, call GET /templates/archive-product/verify. On failure the response includes probable_causes and next_steps (force-refresh, /cache/flush, Cloudflare purge).
set_template_archive-product._doc: PIXEL-PERFECT archive-product (shop page). Pure isolation — no WC scripts needed for the listing grid itself (unless you add quick-buy).
set_template_archive-product.code:
> Shop — <?php bloginfo( 'name' ); ?>
get_image( 'medium_large' ); ?>

get_price_html(); ?>

set_template_single-post._flag_persistence: Flags inherit from prior saved state when omitted. Pass reset_flags:true to force defaults. Response includes flag_sources map so you can see exactly which value source each flag used.
set_template_single-post._layout_injections: v1.59.2: POST your site-wide chrome (header, nav, cart drawer, footer) ONCE to /content/layout-injections with template_slots:["single-post"] — it will auto-appear on every rendered page for this slot. No need to embed chrome in each template.
set_template_single-post._theme_leak_protection: v1.59.2: wp-theme-* body classes and /wp-content/themes/ URLs are auto-stripped in all isolated modes. Pixel-perfect guaranteed.
set_template_single-post._response_note: After POST, call GET /templates/single-post/verify. On failure the response includes probable_causes and next_steps (force-refresh, /cache/flush, Cloudflare purge).
set_template_single-post._doc: PIXEL-PERFECT single blog post. Pure isolation. For comments, set enable_wp_head: true.
set_template_single-post.code:

set_template_front-page._flag_persistence: Flags inherit from prior saved state when omitted. Pass reset_flags:true to force defaults. Response includes flag_sources map so you can see exactly which value source each flag used.
set_template_front-page._layout_injections: v1.59.2: POST your site-wide chrome (header, nav, cart drawer, footer) ONCE to /content/layout-injections with template_slots:["front-page"] — it will auto-appear on every rendered page for this slot. No need to embed chrome in each template.

set_template_front-page._doc: PIXEL-PERFECT front page. Pure isolation — the homepage template, your design, nothing else.
set_template_front-page.code:
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html <?php language_attributes(); ?>>
<head>
<meta charset="utf-8">
<meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1">
<title><?php bloginfo( 'name' ); ?></title>
</head>
<body>
<!-- Your custom homepage here — use $wp_query loops, custom sections, hero, CTAs, etc. -->
</body>
</html>

public_rest_rspd_fields.note: Public WordPress REST responses for RSPD-managed custom post types include a top-level rspd_fields object.

value_shapes.scalar_fields: Text, email, url, number, select, radio, checkbox, true_false, textarea, wysiwyg, date_picker, and color fields return normalized stored values.
value_shapes.image_fields: Image fields return resolved media objects with attachment id, url, alt, title, mime_type, and available sizes.
value_shapes.file_fields: File fields return resolved media objects with attachment id, url, title, mime_type, and filesize when available.
value_shapes.video_fields: Video fields return resolved media objects with attachment id, url, title, mime_type, filesize, and duration metadata when available.
value_shapes.audio_fields: Audio fields return resolved media objects with attachment id, url, title, mime_type, filesize, and duration metadata when available.
value_shapes.embed_fields: Embed fields return normalized objects with the saved url and, when WordPress can resolve it, provider and embed html details.
value_shapes.gallery_fields: Gallery fields return arrays of resolved media objects in saved order.

usage.prefer_rspd_fields: When building listing or detail experiences for RSPD custom post types, prefer rspd_fields over scraping post content.
usage.featured_image_separation: Featured images remain separate from custom image and gallery fields unless a template explicitly maps them.

agent_review_loop.note: When the operator explicitly asks for Telegram notification or Telegram-based review, use the local reputifly-runner.js workflow below. The runner owns the wait phase and blocks the terminal until a human action exists.
agent_review_loop.delivery_channel: Telegram via the chat linked to the default target email in Reputifly API.
agent_review_loop.primary_objective: In Telegram review mode, priority 1 is to obtain an explicit Approve action for the active run. Everything else is subordinate to that loop.

priority_order.1_get_approve_request: Keep the active review run alive until an explicit Approve action is received, or review_timeout closes it.
priority_order.2_answer_or_revise: Handle Ask questions and requested changes only as part of moving the run toward approval.
priority_order.3_never_self_terminate: Do not self-terminate just because implementation work seems complete, a summary was sent, or a local response ended.

agent_review_loop.success_condition: A reviewed run counts as complete only when the local runner exits with approved or review_timeout.
agent_review_loop.hard_rule: Once Telegram review mode is active, never stop just because the work is done, a response was sent, or a local turn ended. Run the local runner, let it hold the terminal in the foreground, and only resume when it exits with a terminal stdout result. In normal use that means waiting for Approve; the only automatic fallback stop is review_timeout.
agent_review_loop.runner_note: Prompt guidance alone is not enough for persistent review. Use the local foreground runner so the terminal stays blocked until the human presses Ask, Changes, Approve, or the review timeout is reached.
agent_review_loop.ask_mode: Ask is non-mutating. It is for questions only and should return the run to review mode after the answer is sent.

response_policy.local_turns_are_not_terminal: Do not treat the end of a single model response or chat turn as the end of the review task.
response_policy.notify_then_resume_waiting: After each completed implementation cycle or Ask answer, run the local runner command again and let it resume the wait phase.
response_policy.only_terminal_actions: Only Approve or review_timeout can end the review loop. Ask and Changes always keep the run alive.

workflow.bootstrap_if_missing: If reputifly-runner.js is missing and Telegram review mode was explicitly requested, run the bootstrap download command first. If bootstrap fails, stop and ask the operator to place reputifly-runner.js in the working directory manually.
workflow.write_notify_input: After completing an implementation cycle, write a small notify JSON file with title, summary, optional details, and optional links.
workflow.run_notify_command: Run node reputifly-runner.js notify --profile <profile.json> --input <notify.json>. The terminal must stay blocked until the runner exits.
workflow.parse_stdout: When the runner exits, parse only the JSON inside the stdout sentinel block and react to the action value.
workflow.changes_requested: If stdout action is changes_requested, implement the requested changes, then run the notify command again.
workflow.ask_question: If stdout action is ask_requested, write a small answer JSON file, run node reputifly-runner.js answer --profile <profile.json> --input <answer.json>, and let the runner block again.
workflow.approved_stops_work: If stdout action is approved, stop the review loop for that run.
workflow.review_timeout: If stdout action is review_timeout, stop the review loop for that run because the human was idle too long.

local_runner.required_placement: Place reputifly-runner.js in the same working directory as the profile JSON and the agent session.
local_runner.main_directive: When Telegram review mode is requested, use only the local reputifly-runner.js commands below. The runner owns all waiting and review communication for the review phase.
local_runner.missing_runner_behavior: If reputifly-runner.js is not present in the working directory, first try the bootstrap download command below. If bootstrap fails, stop and report that the local runner file is missing instead of attempting to reimplement the review loop ad hoc.

bootstrap.when_to_use: Use only when Telegram review mode was explicitly requested and reputifly-runner.js is missing locally.
bootstrap.fallback_rule: If the bootstrap download fails, stop and ask the operator to place reputifly-runner.js in the working directory manually.

actions.approved: The human approved the run. Stop.
actions.changes_requested: Implement the requested changes, then run the notify command again.
actions.ask_requested: Prepare an answer JSON file, run the answer command, then let the runner block again.
actions.error: Local runner failure. Read stderr and the JSON text field before retrying.

result_shape.text: Human Ask or Changes text when applicable

code_field_encoding.note: All endpoints that accept code/HTML content (templates, layout injections, robots.txt, page overrides) support base64-encoded alternatives to avoid JSON escaping issues.
code_field_encoding.usage: For any field like "code", "header_html", "footer_html", "body_html", "content", you can send "{field_name}_base64" instead with the base64-encoded value. Example: {"code_base64": "PD9waHAg..."} instead of {"code": "<?php ..."}.
code_field_encoding.benefit: Eliminates backslash stripping, quote escaping, and special character corruption in code payloads. Recommended for templates with PHP, complex HTML, or inline JavaScript.
code_field_encoding.template_file_endpoint: GET /templates/{slot}/file returns the raw PHP file from disk including baked-in headers/footers, plus a file_content_base64 field for lossless round-tripping.
